FARNBOROUGH AIRPORT IS EQUIPPED WITH AN ADS-B SURFACE SURVEILLANCE SYSTEM (UNDER TRIAL). 1/ FLIGHT CREW SHOULD SELECT XPDR, OR EQUIVALENT, AUTO IF AVAILABLE, AND THE ASSIGNED CODE: A/ FROM THE REQUEST TO PUSH BACK OR TAXI, WHICHEVER IS EARLIER. B/ AFTER LANDING, CONTINUOUSLY UNTIL THE AIRCRAFT IS PARKED ON STAND. AFTER PARKING THE MODE A CODE 2000 MUST BE SET BEFORE SELECTING OFF OR STDBY. 2/ FLIGHT CREW OF AIRCRAFT EQUIPPED WITH MODE S HAVING AN AIRCRAFT IDENTIFICATION FEATURE SHOULD ALSO SET THE AIRCRAFT IDENTIFICATION. THIS SETTING IS THE AIRCRAFT IDENTIFICATION SPECIFIED IN ITEM 7 OF THE ICAO ATC FLIGHT PLAN. THE AIRCRAFT IDENTIFICATION SHOULD BE ENTERED FROM THE REQUEST FOR PUSH BACK OR TAXI, WHICHEVER IS EARLIER, THROUGH THE FMS OR THE TRANSPONDER CONTROL PANEL. NOTAM WILL BE SUPERSEDED BY AIP SUP WEF 28 MAY 2026.

HUMBERSIDE ATZ ACTIVATION AND AD HR AS FOLLOWS: MON 0500-0530 (PPR), 0630-1700, 1945-2045 (PPR) TUE-FRI 0500-0530 (PPR), 0630-0730, 0800-1430, 1515-1700, 1945-2045 (PPR) SAT-SUN 0500-0530 (PPR), 0800-1430, 1515-1700, 1945-2045 (PPR) ATS AVBL DURING ABOVE AD OPR HR ONLY. NO ARR OR DEP LATER THAN CLOSING TIMES MINUS 15 MINS. INBD ACFT ARE REQUIRED TO ALLOW SUFFICIENT TIME TO RECEIVE AN ATS AFTER AD REOPENING. SAR OPS OCCUR H24, PILOTS TRANSITING THROUGH OR WI THE VCY OF THE ATZ OUTSIDE OF THE AD OPR HR ARE RECOMMENDED TO MAKE BLIND CALLS HUMBERSIDE TWR CH 124.905. USE OF THE AD DURING PPR PERIODS IS BY PRIOR ARRANGEMENT THROUGH A HANDLING AGENT OR WITH TERMINAL SUPERVISOR ON 07718 524072.

ADDITIONAL PROCEDURES ARE IN PLACE TO REGULATE TRAFFIC FLOW AT ISLE OF MAN AIRPORT DURING THE ISLE OF MAN TT RACES. ALL VISITING AIRCRAFT WILL REQUIRE A HANDLING AGENT AND TO SUBMIT A FULL FLIGHT PLAN. A SLOT REFERENCE NUMBER WILL ALSO BE REQUIRED ON NOMINATED DATES. AIRCRAFT FREECALLING ATC WITHOUT THESE MAY NOT BE PERMITTED TO LAND. AIRCRAFT MAY ALSO BE ASKED TO REMAIN OUTSIDE CONTROLLED AIRSPACE SUBJECT TO CONTROLLER WORKLOAD. BFS038/2026 REFERS

ADDITIONAL PROCEDURES ARE IN PLACE WITHIN ISLE OF MAN CTR DURING THE ISLE OF MAN TT RACES. ALL ACFT WILL REQUIRE A FULL FLIGHT PLAN. ACFT MAY ALSO BE ASKED TO REMAIN OUTSIDE CONTROLLED AIRSPACE SUBJECT TO CONTROLLER WORKLOAD. ACFT OPR INTO MOUNT RULE SHOULD PLAN TO ROUTE AROUND THE CTR TO REQUEST ENTRY VIA LAXEY HARBOUR VRP. BFS 033/2026 REFERS.

TRIGGER NOTAM - AIP SUP 038/26 WEF 27 MAY 26 UNTIL 27 AUG 26 - TEMPORARY DANGER AREA (NORTHERN NORTH SEA) BVLOS OPERATIONS

WITH EFFECT FROM 13 MAY 26 AT 1600, THE UK NOF WILL ISSUE NOTAM ON BEHALF OF THE TURKS AND CAICOS ISLANDS (MB) UNDER OUR UNIFORM SERIES. THE U SERIES DESCRIPTION IN THE UK NOTAM SERIES TABLE WILL BE AMENDED IN DUE COURSE. UK AIP GEN 3.1 REFERS

THE NOTAM OFFICE WILL BE UNABLE TO ISSUE NOTAM/SNOWTAM DUE TO A PLANNED EUROPEAN AIS DATABASE (EAD) OUTAGE. DURING THIS OUTAGE, ONLY TIME CRITICAL NOTAM/SNOWTAM WILL BE TRANSMITTED AS AN X SERIES NOTAM/SNOWTAM IF SENT TO EGGNYNYX. ADDITIONALLY, PIB GENERATION AND ACCESS TO THE UK AIP VIA THE AIS INTERNET BRIEFING SERVICE (IBS) WILL NOT BE AVBL. THEY WILL HOWEVER, BE AVBL VIA OUR CONTINGENCY SITE USING THE FOLLOWING ADDRESS - HTTPS://WWW.NATS.AERO/DO-IT-ONLINE/PRE-FLIGHT-INFORMATION-BULLETINS/)

SECURITY - HAZARDOUS SITUATION IN LIBYA UK REGISTERED AIR OPERATORS ARE RECOMMENDED NOT TO ENTER FIR TRIPOLI (HLLL). POTENTIAL RISK FROM ANTI-AIRCRAFT WEAPONRY. THIS SUPERSEDES THE ADVICE IN THE CURRENT UK AIP. UK AIP ENR 1.1. PARA 1.4.5 REFERS.

SECURITY - HAZARDOUS SITUATION IN AFGHANISTAN. UK REGISTERED AIR OPERATORS ARE ADVISED TO TAKE POTENTIAL RISK INTO ACCOUNT WITHIN FIR KABUL (OAKX). POTENTIAL RISK FROM HEIGHTENED MILITARY ACTIVITY. AR-2026-1616/01.

SECURITY - HAZARDOUS SITUATION IN LEBANON. UK REGISTERED AIR OPERATORS ARE RECOMMENDED NOT TO ENTER FIR BEIRUT (OLBB). POTENTIAL RISK FROM HEIGHTENED MILITARY ACTIVITY. FOR MORE INFO CONTACT UK DEPARTMENT FOR TRANSPORT 02070 826639 OR 02079 443111 OUT OF HOURS. AR-2026-3194/01.

SECURITY - HAZARDOUS SITUATION IN MALI. UK CIVIL AIR OPERATORS ARE RECOMMENDED NOT TO ENTER FIR NIAMEY (DRRR) AND FIR DAKAR (GOOO) WITHIN THE TERRITORY AND AIRSPACE OF MALI BELOW 25,000 FT ABOVE GROUND LEVEL (AGL). POTENTIAL RISK FROM ANTI-AIRCRAFT WEAPONRY AND HEIGHTENED MILITARY ACTIVITY. THIS SUPERSEDES THE ADVICE IN THE CURRENT UK AIP ENR 1.1. AR-2026-3729/01.
